query: Who led the first high-level US government delegation/flight to Cuba (outside Guantánamo) since 2016, and on what exact date — a senior State Department delegation in mid-April 2026, or CIA Director John Ratcliffe on May 14, 2026? Clarify whether these were two distinct visits and which was actually first.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is the summary:

## Answer

These were **two distinct visits**, and the **senior State Department delegation was clearly first**.

**First (April 10, 2026):** A senior **State Department delegation**, dispatched under Secretary of State **Marco Rubio**, flew to Havana on a US government plane. This was explicitly the first US government aircraft to land in Cuba — outside Guantánamo — since Obama's 2016 visit. It went public via an Axios scoop (April 17) and CBS/CNN/NPR coverage April 20–21. Members weren't named publicly (reported as assistant secretaries meeting Cuban deputy ministers); one official met Raúl Castro's grandson, Raúl Guillermo Rodríguez Castro.

**Second (May 14, 2026):** **CIA Director John Ratcliffe** traveled to Havana ~5 weeks later — reported as only the *second* officially recognized US government flight to Cuba since 2016. He met Cuba's interior minister, intelligence chief, and Rodríguez Castro, delivering a message conditioning expanded engagement on "fundamental changes."

**Bottom line:** The "first high-level US government delegation/flight to Cuba (outside Guantánamo) since 2016" was the **State Department delegation on April 10, 2026** — *not* Ratcliffe on May 14. Ratcliffe's was a separate, higher-profile visit (led personally by the CIA director), but it came second.
